message:
fix(musl): enable vendored-openssl on git2 so musl builds link cleanly

The new x86_64-unknown-linux-musl matrix entry (236319a9) compiles fine
but fails to link because openssl-sys can't find a musl-compatible OpenSSL
on the host. git2 already pulled in openssl-sys via vendored-libgit2's
HTTPS transport.

Adding the vendored-openssl feature tells openssl-sys to compile OpenSSL
from source via openssl-src (added to Cargo.lock). No behavioural change
on gnu/darwin/windows — it just removes the runtime OpenSSL dep and adds
~1-2 min to CI build time on every target.

Unblocks: #101

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -5545,6 +5545,15 @@ version = "0.2.1"
 source = "registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index"
 checksum = "7c87def4c32ab89d880effc9e097653c8da5d6ef28e6b539d313baaacfbafcbe"
 
+[[package]]
+name = "openssl-src"
+version = "300.6.0+3.6.2"
+source = "registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index"
+checksum = "a8e8cbfd3a4a8c8f089147fd7aaa33cf8c7450c4d09f8f80698a0cf093abeff4"
+dependencies = [
+ "cc",
+]
+
 [[package]]
 name = "openssl-sys"
 version = "0.9.114"
@@ -5553,6 +5562,7 @@ checksum = "13ce1245cd07fcc4cfdb438f7507b0c7e4f3849a69fd84d52374c66d83741bb6"
 dependencies = [
  "cc",
  "libc",
+ "openssl-src",
  "pkg-config",
  "vcpkg",
 ]

@@ -81,7 +81,7 @@ ammonia = "4.1.2"
 regex = "1"
 unicode-normalization = "0.1"
 similar = "2"
-git2 = { version = "0.20", features = ["vendored-libgit2"] }
+git2 = { version = "0.20", features = ["vendored-libgit2", "vendored-openssl"] }
 glob = "0.3.3"
 globset = "0.4"
 tracing = "0.1"
